cards.back: E(X) = np
Var(X) = np(1 - p)
P(X = k) = e^(-λ) · λ^k / k!
E(X) = Var(X) = λ
P(A|B) = P(B|A) · P(A) / P(B)
P(B) = Σ P(B|A_i) · P(A_i) over all partitions A_i
68% within ±1σ, 95% within ±2σ, 99.7% within ±3σ
Z = (X - μ) / σ
E(X) = Σ x_i · P(x_i)
Var(X) = E(X²) - [E(X)]²
P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B) / P(B)
P(A ∩ B) = P(A) · P(B) iff A and B are independent
E(X) = 1/p (number of trials until first success)
Var(X) = (b - a)² / 12
Cov(X,Y) = E(XY) - E(X)·E(Y)
ρ = Cov(X,Y) / (σ_X · σ_Y), range [-1, 1]
P(X ≥ a) ≤ E(X) / a, for X ≥ 0
P(|X - μ| ≥ kσ) ≤ 1/k²
F(x) = 1 - e^(-λx) for x ≥ 0, Mean = 1/λ
T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n). If f(n) = O(n^(log_b(a) - ε)), then T(n) = Θ(n^(log_b(a)))
If f(n) = Θ(n^(log_b(a))), then T(n) = Θ(n^(log_b(a)) · log n)
If f(n) = Ω(n^(log_b(a) + ε)) and regularity holds, then T(n) = Θ(f(n))
Time: O(n log n) all cases. Space: O(n). Stable sort.
Best/Avg: O(n log n), Worst: O(n²). Space: O(log n). Not stable.
Time: O(n log n) all cases. Space: O(1). Not stable.
Time: O(n + k) where k = range. Space: O(k). Stable.
Time: O(log n). Requires sorted array.
O((V + E) log V) with adjacency list + binary heap
O(V · E). Handles negative weights. Detects negative cycles.
O(V³). All-pairs shortest path. Works with negative weights (no neg cycles).
O(E log E). Sort edges, use Union-Find. Greedy.
O((V + E) log V) with binary heap.
O(V + E) with adjacency list.
O(V + E) with adjacency list.
O(V + E). Only for DAGs. Uses DFS or Kahn's (BFS with in-degree).
Time: O(nW), Space: O(nW). n = items, W = capacity.
Time: O(mn). DP table: if match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1]+1; else max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).
Time: O(n³). Minimize scalar multiplications for matrix product.
Ω(n log n). Any comparison-based sort needs at least n log n comparisons in worst case.
AMAT = Hit Time + (Miss Rate × Miss Penalty)
EAT = h × (TLB_time + Mem_time) + (1-h) × (TLB_time + 2 × Mem_time), h = TLB hit rate
EAT = (1-p) × memory_access + p × page_fault_time, p = page fault rate
CPU Utilization = 1 - p^n, p = I/O fraction, n = degree of multiprogramming
TAT = Completion Time - Arrival Time
WT = Turnaround Time - Burst Time
RT = First Run Time - Arrival Time
Throughput = Number of processes completed / Total time
Need[i] = Max[i] - Allocation[i]. System is safe if a safe sequence exists.
Mutual Exclusion, Hold & Wait, No Preemption, Circular Wait — all four must hold.
R ≥ P × (N-1) + 1, P = processes, N = max need per process
Total Seek = Σ |current_track - next_track| for all requests in order
Virtual Addr = [Page Number | Page Offset]. Page# = addr / page_size, Offset = addr % page_size
Entries = 2^(virtual address bits - offset bits) = Virtual Address Space / Page Size
wait(S): if S>0 then S-- else block. signal(S): S++ (or wake a blocked process).
d_prop = Distance / Propagation Speed
d_trans = Packet Size (L) / Bandwidth (R)
d_total = d_prop + d_trans + d_queue + d_proc
Throughput = L / (RTT + L/R)
Efficiency = (L/R) / (RTT + L/R) = 1 / (1 + 2a), a = d_prop / d_trans
Efficiency = min(1, W / (1 + 2a)), W = window size
W ≤ 2^n - 1, n = number of sequence bits
W ≤ 2^(n-1), n = number of sequence bits
Usable hosts = 2^(32 - prefix) - 2 (subtract network and broadcast)
C = B × log₂(1 + SNR), B = bandwidth in Hz
Max Rate = 2B × log₂(L), B = bandwidth, L = signal levels
L_min = 2 × d_prop × R (so sender detects collision before finishing)
Efficiency = 1 / (1 + a/N), a = d_prop/d_trans, N = stations
Throughput ≈ (MSS / RTT) × (1 / √p), p = loss rate
2^r ≥ m + r + 1, m = data bits, r = parity bits
Speedup = n × k / (k + n - 1), n = instructions, k = stages
Throughput = n / ((k + n - 1) × cycle_time)
CPI = Σ (CPI_i × F_i), F_i = fraction of instruction type i
CPU Time = Instruction Count × CPI × Clock Cycle Time
MIPS = Clock Rate / (CPI × 10^6)
Speedup = 1 / ((1 - f) + f/S), f = fraction enhanced, S = enhancement speedup
32 bits: 1 sign, 8 exponent (bias 127), 23 mantissa. Range ≈ ±1.18×10^-38 to ±3.4×10^38
64 bits: 1 sign, 11 exponent (bias 1023), 52 mantissa
(A + B)' = A' · B' and (A · B)' = A' + B'
2^(2^n) functions of n variables
Number of lines = Cache Size / Block Size
Sets = Cache Size / (Block Size × Associativity). Tag bits = Address bits - Index bits - Offset bits
Effective bandwidth increases by factor m (m-way interleaving), access time overlap
Signed multiplication. Check pairs of bits: 00/11 = shift, 01 = add, 10 = subtract.
All attributes are atomic (no multi-valued or composite attributes).
1NF + no partial dependency (no non-prime attribute depends on part of a candidate key).
2NF + no transitive dependency. For every FD X→A: X is superkey OR A is prime.
For every non-trivial FD X→A, X must be a superkey. Stricter than 3NF.
Internal: ⌈m/2⌉ to m children, leaf: ⌈(m-1)/2⌉ to m-1 keys. Root: at least 2 children.
h ≤ ⌈log_⌈m/2⌉((N+1)/2)⌉, N = number of keys
Atomicity (all or nothing), Consistency (valid states), Isolation (concurrent txns don't interfere), Durability (committed = permanent).
Growing phase: acquire locks. Shrinking phase: release locks. Guarantees conflict-serializability.
Build precedence graph. If acyclic, schedule is conflict-serializable.
σ (sigma) = selection (rows). π (pi) = projection (columns).
O(n × m) in worst case. With index: O(n × log m).
Find attributes not in RHS of any FD → must be in every key. Compute closure to verify.
R1 ∩ R2 → R1 or R1 ∩ R2 → R2 must hold (common attributes form a key of one part).
A decomposition is dependency-preserving if all FDs can be checked within individual decomposed relations.
Dense: one entry per record. Sparse: one entry per block. Sparse only on ordering field.
Recursively Enumerable. Recognized by Turing Machine. No restrictions on productions.
Context-Sensitive. |LHS| ≤ |RHS|. Recognized by Linear Bounded Automaton.
Context-Free. LHS is single non-terminal. Recognized by Pushdown Automaton.
Regular. A → aB or A → a. Recognized by Finite Automaton.
For regular L, ∃ p: ∀ s ∈ L with |s| ≥ p, s = xyz where |xy| ≤ p, |y| ≥ 1, xy^iz ∈ L ∀ i ≥ 0.
∃ p: ∀ s with |s| ≥ p, s = uvxyz, |vxy| ≤ p, |vy| ≥ 1, uv^ixy^iz ∈ L ∀ i ≥ 0.
Closed under: union, intersection, complement, concatenation, Kleene star, reversal.
Closed under: union, concatenation, Kleene star. NOT closed under: intersection, complement.
CFL ∩ Regular = CFL (intersection of a CFL with a regular language is always a CFL).
Emptiness, finiteness, membership for Regular & CFL are decidable.
Halting problem, Post Correspondence Problem, ambiguity of CFG, whether CFL = Σ* are undecidable.
Every non-trivial property of the language of a Turing machine is undecidable.
Use Myhill-Nerode theorem or table-filling algorithm. Minimum DFA is unique.
Subset construction. Worst case: 2^n states from n-state NFA.
Multi-tape, non-deterministic, multi-track TMs all equivalent in power to standard TM.
Access: O(1). Search: O(n). Insert at end: O(1) amortized. Insert at pos: O(n). Delete: O(n).

cards.front: Linked List: Access / Search / Insert / Delete
cards.back: Access: O(n). Search: O(n). Insert at head: O(1). Insert at pos: O(n). Delete given node: O(1).
Push: O(1). Pop: O(1). Peek: O(1). LIFO order.
Enqueue: O(1). Dequeue: O(1). FIFO order.
Search: O(log n). Insert: O(log n). Delete: O(log n). Worst (skewed): O(n).
Search: O(log n). Insert: O(log n). Delete: O(log n). Height-balanced (|BF| ≤ 1).
Search: O(log n). Insert: O(log n). Delete: O(log n). Max height: 2·log₂(n+1).
Search: O(log n). Insert: O(log n). Delete: O(log n). Disk-optimized.
Insert: O(log n). Extract-Min/Max: O(log n). Build Heap: O(n). Peek: O(1).
Average: Insert O(1), Search O(1), Delete O(1). Worst (all collisions): O(n).
α = n/m (items/slots). Expected chain length = α. Rehash when α exceeds threshold.
Insert: O(L). Search: O(L). Delete: O(L). L = length of key. Space: O(ALPHABET × N × L).
Matrix: Space O(V²), edge check O(1). List: Space O(V+E), edge check O(degree).
With union by rank + path compression: nearly O(1) amortized (α(n) inverse Ackermann).
Build: O(n). Query: O(log n). Update: O(log n). Space: O(n).
Build: O(n log n). Query prefix sum: O(log n). Update: O(log n). Space: O(n).
Search: O(log n) expected. Insert: O(log n) expected. Space: O(n) expected.
Insert front/back: O(1). Remove front/back: O(1).
Insert: O(log n). Extract-Min/Max: O(log n). Decrease-Key: O(log n).

cards.front: Number of BSTs with n nodes
cards.back: Catalan number: C(n) = C(2n, n) / (n+1) = (2n)! / ((n+1)! × n!)
A* is optimal if heuristic h(n) is admissible (never overestimates) and consistent (h(n) ≤ c(n,n') + h(n')).
BFS: Time O(b^d), Space O(b^d). DFS: Time O(b^m), Space O(bm). b=branching, d=depth, m=max depth.
Error = Bias² + Variance + Noise. High bias = underfitting. High variance = overfitting.
Precision = TP / (TP + FP). Of all predicted positives, how many are actually positive.
Recall = TP / (TP + FN). Of all actual positives, how many were correctly predicted.
F1 = 2 × (Precision × Recall) / (Precision + Recall). Harmonic mean of precision and recall.
IG(S, A) = Entropy(S) - Σ (|Sv|/|S|) × Entropy(Sv). Measures reduction in entropy after split.
Gini(S) = 1 - Σ p_i². Used in CART. Gini = 0 means pure node.
Margin = 2 / ||w||. SVM maximizes this margin. Support vectors lie on the margin boundary.
Features are conditionally independent given the class. P(x1,...,xn|C) = Π P(xi|C).
Minimize Σ Σ ||x - μ_k||² (within-cluster sum of squares). Converges but may find local optimum.
Find directions (principal components) of maximum variance. Project data onto top-k eigenvectors of covariance matrix.
σ(x) = 1 / (1 + e^(-x)). Output range (0,1). Suffers from vanishing gradient.
f(x) = max(0, x). Solves vanishing gradient. Can cause dead neurons (output always 0).
Uses chain rule to compute ∂Loss/∂w for each weight. Gradient flows backward from output to input layers.
softmax(z_i) = e^(z_i) / Σ e^(z_j). Converts logits to probabilities summing to 1. Used in multi-class.
Gradients shrink exponentially in deep networks with sigmoid/tanh. Solutions: ReLU, LSTM, residual connections.
Convolutional layers learn spatial features via filters. Parameters shared across spatial locations (translation invariance).
States, observations, transition probabilities, emission probabilities, initial state distribution. Viterbi finds most likely state sequence.
TF-IDF(t,d) = TF(t,d) × log(N / DF(t)). High for terms frequent in doc but rare across corpus.
